title: Metta: Developing Goodwill.To play the media you will need to either update your browser to a recent version or update yourFlash plugin
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and systematic treatment of metta, covering the psychology of conditioning, the nature of attachment versus true love, the four illimitables (metta, compassion, joy, equanimity) with their near and far enemies, and practical guidance on forgiveness. It has excellent literary qualities including clear explanations, memorable examples (Winnie the Pooh, Kisagotami), and a coherent structure that moves from theory to practice. The depth of teaching and natural flow make it ideal as a spine for a chapter on loving-kindness.
summary: This extensive dharma talk systematically explores metta as the foundation of right relationship with ourselves and the world, explaining how loving-kindness, compassion, sympathetic joy, and equanimity function together as the 'divine abodes.' Bhante covers the psychology of conditioning, distinguishes metta from attachment, identifies the subtle enemies of each quality, and provides practical guidance on forgiveness as an essential component of the practice.

relevance.note: Primarily about metta practice and developing universal goodwill
